text: Children as a category have been recognized as a special group in modern society in both criminal law and criminology. The ongoing transformation of social relations and the resulting adjustment of the normative and institutional framework to children’s specific needs are among the factors that have caused children to be dealt with differently in criminal law. As part of the repressive apparatus of the state – criminal and misdemeanor law – children are dealt with according to the principles of the modern juvenile criminal law, referred to today as the child in conflict with the law. This paper gives an overview of the current standards for the legal status of children and provides insights into their implementation at the national level. Besides dealing with the child as an offender, we shall also focus on the position of the child as a crime victim.
